# Frameze Components

> Open-source registry of Frameze's Framer components. Built for both human developers and AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, v0, etc.) that need to discover, evaluate, and use a component programmatically.

Frameze is a Framer component/template marketplace at https://frameze.com. This repository is the open-source source of truth for component code — the marketplace site is the storefront, this repo is where the actual code lives.

## Start here

- `registry/registry.json` — machine-readable index of every component in this repo. Each entry points to a `component.json` manifest and the component's source file.
- `registry/design-tokens.json` — the paper/ink/glass theme vocabulary used across components, for agents that need to generate visually-consistent variants.

## Per-component structure

Every component lives in `components/<slug>/` with exactly three files:

- `<Name>.tsx` — the real, working Framer code component source.
- `component.json` — machine-readable manifest: props, dependencies, category, "when to use this", and marketplace metadata (price/preview/buy link if it's also sold on frameze.com).
- `README.md` — human-readable docs: when to use it, install steps, a prop table, and notes on implementation details worth knowing before you copy it.

To use a component: read its `component.json` for the prop schema, then read the `.tsx` file directly — it's the real source, not a stub.

## For AI agents

- All components are plain React/TypeScript Framer code components (`export default function ComponentName(props) { ... }` + `addPropertyControls`). No build step, no bundler config — copy the file into a Framer Code Component and it works.
- `component.json`'s `props` field is a flattened summary; the authoritative prop list is the `addPropertyControls` call at the bottom of each `.tsx` file.
- `component.json`'s `whenToUse` field exists specifically to help you decide relevance to a user's request without reading the full source first.
- License is MIT — safe to use, adapt, and suggest in generated code.
- If your host supports MCP, connect to the `frameze` server (see `mcp/README.md`) and call `search_components` / `get_component_code` / `list_design_tokens` directly instead of fetching raw files.

## Roadmap

More components are added over time (see `registry/registry.json` for the current list). The `mcp/` server is live, reading this registry directly from GitHub on every call — no redeploy needed as new components are added. Next up: publishing it to npm as `frameze-mcp` for one-line install.
